title: 3-Day Kathmandu Heritage Immersion

daily_plan.title: Ancient Durbar Squares & Sacred Temples
daily_plan.morning: Visit Kathmandu Durbar Square — explore Hanuman Dhoka palace, the living goddess Kumari's residence, and Kasthamandap temple that gave Kathmandu its name.
daily_plan.afternoon: Walk through the narrow alleys of Asan and Indra Chowk bazaars. Try local street food — chatamari (Newari pizza) and juju dhau (king yogurt) from clay pots.
daily_plan.evening: Witness the evening aarti ceremony at Pashupatinath Temple on the banks of the Bagmati River. Watch the cremation ghats and sadhus in meditation.

daily_plan.title: Buddhist Heritage & Panoramic Views
daily_plan.morning: Climb the 365 steps to Swayambhunath (Monkey Temple) at sunrise for panoramic views of the entire Kathmandu Valley. Spin the massive prayer wheels.
daily_plan.afternoon: Explore Boudhanath Stupa — the largest spherical stupa in Nepal. Join monks in the kora (circumambulation) walk and visit small Tibetan monasteries surrounding the stupa.
daily_plan.evening: Dinner in Thamel district — try traditional Newari feast (samay baji) at a heritage restaurant. Explore the vibrant nightlife and live music scene.

daily_plan.title: Patan's Artisan Legacy
daily_plan.morning: Visit Patan Durbar Square — admire the stone Krishna Mandir, golden Patan Museum (Nepal's finest museum), and intricate Newari woodcarvings on every temple strut.
daily_plan.afternoon: Explore the Patan metalworking quarter where artisans still cast bronze Buddha statues using the lost-wax method passed down for centuries. Buy authentic handicrafts.
daily_plan.evening: Sunset at Bhaktapur's Nyatapola Temple if time permits, or farewell dinner at a rooftop restaurant overlooking the Kathmandu skyline.

highlights: Seven UNESCO World Heritage Sites in one valley

daily_plan.title: Lakeside Arrival & Sunset Views
daily_plan.morning: Arrive in Pokhara by tourist bus or flight. Check in at a Lakeside hotel with mountain views. Rest and adjust to the relaxed vibe.
daily_plan.afternoon: Hire a colorful boat on Phewa Lake and row to Tal Barahi Temple on the island. Enjoy the reflections of Machhapuchhre (Fishtail Peak) in the water.
daily_plan.evening: Walk the Lakeside promenade, browse shops, and enjoy wood-fired pizza or Thakali thali at a rooftop restaurant watching the sunset over the Annapurna range.

daily_plan.title: Paragliding Over the Himalayas
daily_plan.morning: Drive to Sarangkot for a tandem paragliding flight (30-minute) soaring above Phewa Lake with a 360-degree panorama of Annapurna, Dhaulagiri, and Machhapuchhre peaks.
daily_plan.afternoon: Visit the International Mountain Museum to learn about Himalayan mountaineering history. See climbing gear from historic expeditions.
daily_plan.evening: Try Pokhara's famous fish from the lake — grilled rainbow trout — at a Lakeside restaurant. Optional: visit a jazz bar.

daily_plan.title: Poon Hill Sunrise Trek — Day 1
daily_plan.morning: Drive to Nayapul and begin the trek to Tikhedhunga through terraced rice paddies and Magar villages. Gradual uphill through subtropical forest.
daily_plan.afternoon: Continue climbing stone steps to Ulleri (2,070m). Tough staircase but rewarded with mountain views. Arrive at Ghorepani by late afternoon.
daily_plan.evening: Rest at a teahouse lodge. Hot dal bhat dinner and early sleep for the 4:30 AM sunrise wake-up call.

daily_plan.title: Poon Hill Sunrise & Return
daily_plan.morning: Wake at 4:30 AM and hike 45 minutes to Poon Hill (3,210m) for one of Nepal's most famous sunrises — golden light illuminating Dhaulagiri, Annapurna South, and Machhapuchhre.
daily_plan.afternoon: Descend through rhododendron forests (spectacular if visiting Mar-Apr when they bloom in red and pink). Pass through Birethanti village back to Nayapul.
daily_plan.evening: Return to Pokhara Lakeside. Celebrate with a well-earned steak dinner and cold beer at a Lakeside grill.

daily_plan.title: Caves, Falls & Farewell
daily_plan.morning: Visit Davis Falls (Patale Chhango) — a waterfall that disappears into an underground tunnel. Then explore the adjacent Gupteshwor Mahadev cave with its stalactites and hidden Shiva shrine.
daily_plan.afternoon: Zip-line adventure (one of the world's longest at 1.8 km) or a peaceful yoga session at a Lakeside studio. Last-minute shopping for Tibetan singing bowls.
daily_plan.evening: Farewell dinner at a rooftop restaurant with live Nepali folk music. Watch Machhapuchhre turn pink in the alpenglow.

title: 14-Day Everest Base Camp Trek

daily_plan.title: Arrival in Kathmandu
daily_plan.morning: Arrive in Kathmandu. Transfer to Thamel hotel. Gear check and last-minute equipment shopping at the many trekking shops.
daily_plan.afternoon: Visit the Nepal Mountaineering Association for permits. Brief tour of Boudhanath Stupa for pre-trek blessings.
daily_plan.evening: Team dinner and trek briefing with guide. Early rest for the early morning flight.

daily_plan.title: Flight to Lukla & Trek to Phakding
daily_plan.morning: Scenic 35-minute flight from Kathmandu to Lukla (2,860m) — one of the world's most thrilling airport landings on a mountain cliff runway.
daily_plan.afternoon: Begin trekking the Everest trail. Descend through pine forest to Phakding (2,610m) along the Dudh Koshi river. Easy 3-hour walk to acclimatize.
daily_plan.evening: First night in a Sherpa teahouse. Hot tea, dal bhat, and sharing stories with fellow trekkers from around the world.

daily_plan.title: Trek to Namche Bazaar
daily_plan.morning: Cross suspension bridges over the Dudh Koshi river. Steep uphill climb through forest. First glimpse of Everest from a clearing if skies are clear.
daily_plan.afternoon: Arrive at Namche Bazaar (3,440m) — the bustling Sherpa capital carved into a mountain amphitheatre. Explore its colorful market and bakeries.
daily_plan.evening: Enjoy apple pie at a famous Namche bakery. Visit the Sherpa Culture Museum to understand the people who call these mountains home.

daily_plan.title: Acclimatization Day in Namche
daily_plan.morning: Hike up to the Everest View Hotel (3,880m) for a stunning panorama of Everest, Lhotse, and Ama Dablam. Return to Namche for lunch.
daily_plan.afternoon: Explore Namche market day (Saturday). Visit the national park headquarters and mountaineering exhibition. Rest and hydrate.
daily_plan.evening: Watch a documentary about Everest at one of Namche's lodges. Write in your trek journal.

daily_plan.title: Trek to Tengboche
daily_plan.morning: Descend to the Dudh Koshi valley, then climb steadily through rhododendron forest with Ama Dablam towering ahead.
daily_plan.afternoon: Arrive at Tengboche Monastery (3,867m), the most important monastery in the Khumbu. Visit the monastery interior if open for visitors.
daily_plan.evening: If timing is right, attend the monks' evening prayer chanting ceremony. Spectacular sunset views of Everest and Ama Dablam.

daily_plan.title: Trek to Dingboche
daily_plan.morning: Continue up-valley through thinning forest. Cross to the Imja Khola side valley toward Dingboche.
daily_plan.afternoon: Arrive at Dingboche (4,410m). Feel the altitude — walk slowly, drink plenty of water. The landscape becomes stark and high-desert like.
daily_plan.evening: Rest and acclimatize. Simple teahouse dinner. Play cards with fellow trekkers in the dining room warmed by a yak-dung stove.

daily_plan.title: Acclimatization Day in Dingboche
daily_plan.morning: Hike up Nagarjun Hill (5,100m) for an incredible 360-degree panorama. Views of Makalu, Island Peak, and Lhotse south face.
daily_plan.afternoon: Return to Dingboche. Rest, read, and hydrate. Monitor for any altitude sickness symptoms.
daily_plan.evening: Early dinner and sleep. The next days are the most challenging and rewarding of the trek.

daily_plan.title: Trek to Lobuche
daily_plan.morning: Trek along the lateral moraine of the Khumbu Glacier. Pass the memorial cairns for fallen climbers at Thukla Pass.
daily_plan.afternoon: Arrive at Lobuche (4,940m). Barren, rocky landscape with massive glaciers and towering peaks on every side.
daily_plan.evening: Early dinner. Temperatures drop well below freezing. Bundle up in your sleeping bag and rest for the big day tomorrow.

daily_plan.title: Everest Base Camp!
daily_plan.morning: Early start for Gorak Shep (5,164m). Drop bags and continue to Everest Base Camp (5,364m). Walk on the Khumbu Glacier moraine.
daily_plan.afternoon: Reach EBC! Stand where legendary expeditions began. See the Khumbu Icefall glittering above. Take photos with the iconic EBC sign. Return to Gorak Shep.
daily_plan.evening: Celebration dinner at Gorak Shep. You made it to the roof of the world's doorstep. Share stories of the achievement.

daily_plan.title: Kala Patthar Sunrise & Descent
daily_plan.morning: Pre-dawn climb to Kala Patthar (5,545m) — the best viewpoint for Everest's full pyramid peak lit by golden sunrise. The highest point of the trek.
daily_plan.afternoon: Descend rapidly to Pheriche (4,371m). Enjoy the thicker air and warmer temperatures. Your body rewards you with energy.
daily_plan.evening: Comfortable teahouse in Pheriche. Reflect on the achievement over hot chocolate.

daily_plan.title: Trek to Namche Bazaar
daily_plan.morning: Continue descent through Tengboche and down to Namche. Enjoy the views you were too breathless to appreciate on the way up.
daily_plan.afternoon: Arrive in Namche. Hot shower (finally!). Shop for souvenirs — Sherpa crafts, prayer flags, and trekking gear at bargain prices.
daily_plan.evening: Celebration dinner at the best restaurant in Namche. Yak steak and local Everest beer.

daily_plan.title: Trek to Lukla
daily_plan.morning: Final day of trekking. Descend through familiar villages and across swinging bridges back to Lukla.
daily_plan.afternoon: Arrive in Lukla. Pack bags for the flight. Explore the small town and buy last-minute gifts.
daily_plan.evening: Farewell dinner with guide and porter team. Exchange contacts and share photos. Tipping ceremony for the crew.

daily_plan.title: Fly Back to Kathmandu
daily_plan.morning: Morning flight back to Kathmandu (weather permitting — always have a buffer day). Scenic mountain views from the plane one last time.
daily_plan.afternoon: Check in to a comfortable Thamel hotel. Long hot shower and fresh clothes. Visit a spa for a well-deserved massage.
daily_plan.evening: Celebration dinner at a fine-dining Nepali restaurant. Try the Nepali tasting menu with local raksi (millet wine).

daily_plan.title: Kathmandu Exploration & Departure
daily_plan.morning: Last-minute sightseeing — Patan Durbar Square or Swayambhunath if not yet visited. Final souvenir shopping in Thamel.
daily_plan.afternoon: Pack and prepare for departure. Transfer to Tribhuvan International Airport.
daily_plan.evening: Depart Nepal with memories of a lifetime and a deep connection to the highest mountains on Earth.

highlights: Standing at Everest Base Camp at 5,364m
Kala Patthar sunrise — best Everest view

title: 10-Day Hidden Nepal — Rara & Upper Mustang

daily_plan.title: Kathmandu — Gateway to Hidden Nepal
daily_plan.morning: Arrive in Kathmandu. Briefing at trekking agency for permits and logistics. Rara requires a special restricted area permit.
daily_plan.afternoon: Explore the hidden corners of Kathmandu — visit the living Kumari of Patan, the secret garden of Garden of Dreams, and the forgotten Itum Bahal monastery.
daily_plan.evening: Welcome dinner featuring dishes from western Nepal — dhido (buckwheat porridge) and gundruk (fermented greens). Briefing for Rara flight.

daily_plan.title: Flight to Talcha & Trek Toward Rara
daily_plan.morning: Morning flight from Kathmandu to Talcha airstrip in Mugu district. Dramatic landing in Nepal's remote far-west. Meet local guide.
daily_plan.afternoon: Begin trek through pristine forest of blue pine, spruce, and juniper. Pass through Thakuri and Magar villages. Gentle climb on forest trails.
daily_plan.evening: Camp or stay at basic lodge in Jhyari village. Home-cooked dal bhat with wild herbs. Stars like you've never seen.

daily_plan.title: Arrival at Rara Lake
daily_plan.morning: Continue trek through alpine meadows carpeted with wildflowers (in season). The air is crisp and utterly silent — no traffic, no crowds.
daily_plan.afternoon: First view of Rara Lake — an impossibly turquoise mirror reflecting snow-capped peaks. Walk along the lakeshore through a canopy of walnut trees.
daily_plan.evening: Set up camp on the lakeshore. Watch the sunset paint the lake gold and pink. Listen to the haunting calls of whooper swans.

daily_plan.title: Full Day at Rara Lake
daily_plan.morning: Circumnavigate Rara Lake on the 14 km trail. Visit the ancient Rara Devi temple and Magar settlement on the far shore.
daily_plan.afternoon: Picnic lunch by the lake. Birdwatching — spot Danphe (Himalayan monal, Nepal's national bird), coots, and eagles.
daily_plan.evening: Campfire stories with local guides. Learn about the Rara legend — locals say the lake was formed from the tears of a goddess.

daily_plan.title: Return Trek & Flight to Nepalgunj
daily_plan.morning: Trek back to Talcha airstrip through different ridge trail with mountain panoramas.
daily_plan.afternoon: Flight from Talcha to Nepalgunj (lowland transit town). Brief stop, then connecting flight or overnight stay.
daily_plan.evening: If overnighting in Nepalgunj, explore the Tharu market and try local fish curry. Prepare for Pokhara/Jomsom transit.

daily_plan.title: Transit to Jomsom — Gateway to Mustang
daily_plan.morning: Flight from Pokhara (or Nepalgunj via Kathmandu connection) to Jomsom along the deepest gorge in the world between Dhaulagiri and Annapurna.
daily_plan.afternoon: Arrive in Jomsom. Explore the windswept Kali Gandaki riverbed. Visit the Mustangi apple orchards and try apple brandy.
daily_plan.evening: Stay at a traditional Thakali lodge. Incredible Thakali set meal — the best regional cuisine in Nepal.

daily_plan.title: Jomsom to Kagbeni — Border of Forbidden Kingdom
daily_plan.morning: Trek north along the Kali Gandaki to Kagbeni — the ancient checkpoint town at the entrance to Upper Mustang. Enter a different world.
daily_plan.afternoon: Explore Kagbeni's medieval alleys. Visit the 500-year-old Kag Chode Thupten Samphel Ling monastery perched on the cliff edge.
daily_plan.evening: Stay in a traditional Mustangi house. Yak butter tea and tsampa (roasted barley flour). Wind howls outside as you sit by the fire.

daily_plan.title: Upper Mustang — Caves & Red Cliffs
daily_plan.morning: Continue north through the red, ochre, and grey desert landscape. Visit the sky caves of Chhoser — ancient cliff dwellings 50 meters up sheer rock faces.
daily_plan.afternoon: Trek through Dhakmar village with its stunning red cliffs. According to legend, the red color is the blood of a demon slain by Guru Rinpoche.
daily_plan.evening: Stay at Ghami village. Visit the longest mani wall in Nepal — a 500-meter wall of carved prayer stones.

daily_plan.title: Lo Manthang — The Walled City
daily_plan.morning: Arrive at Lo Manthang (3,840m) — the walled capital of the ancient Kingdom of Lo. Enter through the single gate in the massive mud-brick walls.
daily_plan.afternoon: Explore inside the walled city — the Raja's palace, Thubchen Gompa with its priceless 15th-century frescoes, Jampa Gompa's massive clay Buddha statue.
daily_plan.evening: Attend a Tibetan Buddhist ceremony if one is being held. Experience the living culture of a kingdom that time forgot.

daily_plan.title: Return via Jomsom & Fly to Pokhara
daily_plan.morning: Begin the return journey south to Jomsom by jeep (8 hours through dramatic desert canyon scenery).
daily_plan.afternoon: Catch late afternoon flight from Jomsom to Pokhara, or overnight in Jomsom if needed.
daily_plan.evening: Arrive back in Pokhara or Kathmandu. Farewell dinner celebrating an extraordinary journey to Nepal's hidden frontiers.

highlights: Rara Lake — Nepal's largest and most remote lake
Walled city of Lo Manthang in Upper Mustang
Zero tourist crowds on the entire route
Living Tibetan Buddhist culture in Mustang
